blockingIssues.description: Repo::save commits the UPSERT transaction before calling hydrate_foreign, then tries to repair failures with db.delete on the parent and tracked foreign rows. That is explicitly the forbidden post-commit compensating-delete design, so the commit boundary is not all-or-nothing as required.

Repo::save_many likewise commits the batch before hydrate_foreign runs, and on failure it issues best-effort deletes for parents and tracked children. This leaves atomicity dependent on cleanup succeeding after commit instead of on the transaction boundary itself.

The new regressions only assert the database is clean after the compensating deletes complete. They do not prove the required stronger contract that the public error path never exposes already-committed residue without relying on post-commit cleanup.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify shared GitNexus fallback guidance so workers may use pure `git diff`-based review when no GitNexus tooling is available at all, and distinguish that case from partial-tool fallback.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback for worker session d16b96e4-3c33-41db-becf-442d4d839435 says no gitnexus_* tools were available; C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md step 2 currently assumes some GitNexus impact/context commands may still be available.

rejectedObservations.observation: The worker reported the feature as already satisfying a true atomic boundary even though the reviewed commit still uses post-commit delete cleanup in save/save_many. The skill/procedure may need a sharper check that commit-boundary atomicity is evidenced in code, not inferred from passing residue-cleanup tests.
